title: cacalia pumila
intro: Cacalia pumila, also known as the small cat's ear or dwarf groundsel, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and flowers have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Cacalia pumila can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used for medicinal purposes.
